comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## WS7 plan-review triage (PASS, 16 advisories) — 2026-07-01

Executing via the user-chosen PROVE-THEN-LOCK sequence: write CONTRIBUTING.md → dogfood it
through Gerrit (proves LLM-Review votes + replication reaches GitHub main) → checkpoint → lock.

Addressed now:
- adv 3,4 (no pre-flight that the LLM-Review bot is up before locking): HANDLED by prove-then-lock
  — the dogfood submit proves the bot votes + replication works BEFORE the lock is applied.
- adv 11 (PR-triggered CI dies post-cutover): NOT a functional break — test/optionality/eval also
  trigger on `push: branches:["**"]`, so CI still runs when Gerrit replicates to main. The
  `pull_request:` trigger becomes vestigial (optional cleanup, noted).
- adv 8,10,14 (doc dangling refs + missing-Change-Id-hook path): authoring CONTRIBUTING.md fresh —
  no dangling 'S7 runbook' ref; will cover the missing-hook non-happy path explicitly.
- adv 13 (file_impact): set.

Surfacing at the PRE-LOCK checkpoint (policy calls for the user):
- adv 1 (CRITICAL): the tag-lock ruleset would reject human `v*` tag pushes → breaks
  .github/workflows/release.yml (PyPI publish on `push: tags`). Options: lock main only (leave tags
  unlocked), or lock tags + push release tags via the deploy key / a CI bypass. Decide before applying.
- adv 16: check/drain open PRs against main before locking. Current open PRs: (see command output).
- adv 7: TF `check` only WARNs (won't abort if deploy key absent) → I'll pre-verify the
  rebar-gerrit-replication key is present (confirmed id 155914477) and use an explicit fail-closed
  pre-check at apply, or convert to a lifecycle precondition.

Deferred as follow-up observability tickets (good hygiene, not blocking the cutover):
- adv 2,9,15 (Terraform drift reconciliation + proving commands), adv 5 (replication-health +
  ruleset-drift alerts), adv 6 (recurring ruleset-active check). Will create discovered_from tickets.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## WS7 + WS6-b COMPLETE — Gerrit-only cutover verified end-to-end (2026-07-01)

Executed prove-then-lock (user-approved), main-only lock (tags left open for releases).

### Deliverables
- **CONTRIBUTING.md** (new) — full Gerrit contributor flow; **CLAUDE.md** git-workflow section
  rewritten (agents push refs/for/main, not GitHub PRs); README links both. LANDED THROUGH GERRIT
  as change 121 (dogfood), not a GitHub PR.
- **Gerrit main seeded** to GitHub main (was 40+1 commits behind; temp admin-push grant → FF →
  revoked; gate restored to pure review-only). Re-seeded after PR #115 landed (per user).
- **GitHub main locked**: created ruleset `gerrit-mirror-lock-main` (id 18402431, deploy-key-only
  `update`+`deletion`+`non_fast_forward`, bypass = DeployKey always); deleted legacy `main-protection`
  (18306946; snapshot saved for rollback). Tags left OPEN (release.yml v* pushes keep working).
- Gerrit project default branch set master → main.

### Proof (WS6-b dogfood)
- change 121: pushed to refs/for/main → review-bot cast LLM-Review over 4 patchsets (findings
  addressed each round; votes correctly dropped on real code changes, re-applied) → +1, 0 unresolved
  → submitted → merged to Gerrit main (a6f690de).
- Replication: FAILED pre-lock ("push declined due to repository rule violations" — legacy ruleset
  blocked the deploy key), SUCCEEDED post-lock → GitHub main advanced to a6f690de (CONTRIBUTING.md
  landed on the mirror as the first Gerrit-replicated change).
- Lock verified: a clean fast-forward human push to GitHub main is REJECTED ("GH013: Cannot update
  this protected ref"); only the replication deploy key can advance main.

### Operational facts / follow-ups
- Break-glass admin: session SSH key at /root/.ssh/gerrit_admin on the box (root-only); admin
  account 1000000 has no HTTP password (SSH-only). JoeOakhartNava (1000007) is admin + has an HTTP pw.
- Rollback: snapshot at $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/legacy-ruleset-18306946.json; re-create legacy or add an
  admin bypass to unlock.
- Deferred (WS7 plan-review advisories, non-blocking): Terraform-github state reconcile + drift
  detection (adv 2,9,15), replication-health + ruleset-drift alerting (adv 5,6). Creating
  discovered_from tickets.

compiled_state.description:
# WS7 — Make Gerrit the only path into GitHub `main` (cutover) + contributor guide

## Why
Complete the dogfood: GitHub `main` must only advance via Gerrit's replication identity, so every
change is gated by the `LLM-Review` review-bot (WS6) before it can land. Today the repo still
carries its legacy branch protection and devs still open GitHub PRs; there is also **no dev-facing
guide** for the Gerrit contribution flow (only setup/ops docs). WS7 flips the switch and documents
the new flow.

## Context — CONSUMES d251/S6 (do not rebuild)
`d251`'s S6 (`075f`, closed) already built the mechanism:
- `infra/terraform-github/` — the repo-level ruleset with a **DeployKey-only bypass** (the
  `rebar-gerrit-replication` key from S5) on `main` (+ tags): `update` (which rejects BOTH direct
  pushes AND PR merges by non-bypass actors) + `deletion` + `non_fast_forward`.
- `infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md` — the cutover & rollback runbook.
- `infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json` — a snapshot of the **pre-existing `main-protection`
  ruleset (id 18048287)** for rollback.

WS7 EXECUTES/finalizes that cutover on `navapbc/rebar` and reconciles any legacy protection — it
does not re-author the ruleset or Terraform.

## What
1. **Remove the legacy protection + activate the Gerrit-only ruleset.** Ensure the pre-existing
   `main-protection` ruleset (18048287) and any other human/PR-merge path is removed/superseded so
   the SOLE ruleset governing `main` is the S6 DeployKey-only lock. Reconcile with the S6 Terraform
   so state matches (no drift, no second competing ruleset).
2. **Contributor guide** — a repo-root `CONTRIBUTING.md` (the doc gap): the Gerrit remote setup, the
   `Change-Id` commit-msg hook, `git push origin HEAD:refs/for/main`, how the single `LLM-Review`
   vote gates submit, how to read a `-1` (a real finding vs a `coverage-gap` infra veto — the WS6
   message tags), how to amend + re-push a patchset, submit, and that **GitHub is a read-only
   mirror** (direct pushes / PR merges are rejected). Linked from `README`.
3. **Live cutover apply + proof** (gated on GitHub admin access): apply the ruleset change and
   verify a direct push / PR-merge to `main` is rejected AND a Gerrit-submitted change replicates
   to GitHub `main`.

## Scope
- IN: reconciling/removing the legacy `main` protection to the single Gerrit-only ruleset; the
  `CONTRIBUTING.md` contributor guide; the live apply + verification.
- OUT: re-authoring S6's ruleset/Terraform/runbook (done); the review-bot decision wiring (WS6);
  provisioning Gerrit/replication (d251).

## Rollback / emergency restore (REQUIRED before the live apply)
Making Gerrit the ONLY path freezes GitHub `main` if the Gerrit gate is later unavailable or
misconfigured (no human can push, no PR can merge). The cutover therefore ships with a
documented, tested rollback that CONSUMES S6's rollback assets:
- **Fast un-lock:** re-enable a human push/merge path by either (a) disabling/deleting the S6
  DeployKey-only ruleset via `gh api -X DELETE /repos/navapbc/rebar/rulesets/{id}` (or
  `terraform destroy` of the ruleset resource), or (b) adding a temporary admin/team `bypass_actor`
  to it — restoring the ability to land a fix directly while Gerrit is repaired.
- **Restore prior protection:** re-apply the pre-existing `main-protection` ruleset from the S6
  snapshot `infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json` (id 18048287) so the repo returns to its
  pre-cutover protection, not to "unprotected".
- **Runbook:** the exact commands + the "when to roll back" trigger live in
  `infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md` (S6's cutover & rollback runbook); WS7 verifies that
  runbook's rollback steps are current for the final ruleset and links them from `CONTRIBUTING.md`
  (so an on-call dev knows the escape hatch). Rollback must be exercised (dry-run/PoC) as part of
  the live apply proof, not merely documented.

## Boundary (honest)
The **live apply** needs a token with `Administration:write` on `navapbc/rebar` and is a
high-impact, mirror-locking change. If that access is not available in this session, the config
reconcile plan + the `CONTRIBUTING.md` are still deliverable; the live apply + proof are surfaced
as blocked (do NOT fake the cutover) — analogous to WS6-b's live-env boundary.

## Acceptance Criteria
### Doable now (no live GitHub admin)
- [ ] A repo-root `CONTRIBUTING.md` documents the full Gerrit contribution flow (remote + Change-Id hook + `push …:refs/for/main` + the `LLM-Review` vote semantics incl. finding-vs-coverage-gap `-1` + amend/re-push + submit + GitHub-is-a-mirror); linked from `README`.
- [ ] The cutover plan names the exact legacy ruleset(s) to remove (incl. `main-protection` id 18048287) and confirms the S6 DeployKey-only ruleset is the sole remaining `main` protection; reconciled against the S6 Terraform (no drift / no competing ruleset).
- [ ] A documented, S6-based **rollback / emergency-restore** procedure is captured (fast un-lock via ruleset delete / temporary bypass_actor, AND restore of the `main-protection` snapshot id 18048287), with a stated "when to roll back" trigger, and linked from `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
### Live-gated (needs `Administration:write` on `navapbc/rebar`)
- [ ] The legacy protection is removed and the Gerrit-only ruleset is active on `navapbc/rebar` `main` (+ tags).
- [ ] The rollback procedure is EXERCISED (dry-run/PoC: un-lock then re-lock) so the escape hatch is proven, not just documented.
- [ ] Proof recorded on this ticket: a direct GitHub push / PR-merge to `main` is REJECTED, and a change submitted in Gerrit replicates to GitHub `main`.

## Dependencies
- CONSUMES d251/S6 (`075f`, closed) tooling + the S5 replication deploy key.
- relates_to WS6 (the review-bot must be voting before Gerrit-only enforcement is meaningful).
compiled_state.title: WS7: Make Gerrit the only path into GitHub main (cutover) + contributor guide
